```json
        }
      ],
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 208 - 218 (Roll no. 167).",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H30000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "15:36:38",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Considered as unfinished business. (consideration: CR H2806-2808)",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H8D000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "15:01:02",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "POSTPONED PROCEEDINGS - At the conclusion of debate on H.R. 4, the Chair put the question on motion to recommit and by voice vote, announced that the noes had prevailed. Ms. Kelly (IL) demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H8A000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "15:00:46",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H36200",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "15:00:36",
      "committees": {
        "item": {
          "name": "Appropriations Committee",
          "systemCode": "hsap00"
        }
      },
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Ms. Kelly (IL) moved to recommit to the Committee on Appropriations. (text: CR H2805)",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H35000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "14:59:58",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H8D000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "13:31:38",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "DEBATE - The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 4.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H8D000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "13:30:50",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 4 with 1 hour of general debate. Motion to recommit allowed. Bill is closed to amendments.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H30000",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionTime": "13:30:45",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 499. (consideration: CR H2796-2805)",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H1L220",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-11",
      "actionTime": "16:51:07",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Rule H. Res. 499 passed House.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H1L210",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-11",
      "actionTime": "09:55:47",
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Rules Committee Resolution H. Res. 499 Reported to House. Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 4 with 1 hour of general debate. Motion to recommit allowed. Bill is closed to amendments.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H11100",
      "actionDate": "2025-06-06",
      "committees": {
        "item": {
          "name": "Appropriations Committee",
          "systemCode": "hsap00"
        }
      },
      "sourceSystem": {
        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Referred to the House Committee on Appropriations.",
```

```json
                  "text": "Amendment SA 2887 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 51. Record Vote Number: 404.",

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 2887 proposed by Senator Booker to Amendment SA 2853. (consideration: CR S4407-4408) To strike the rescission of funds appropriated for the Feed the Future Program to improve global food security and agricultural resiliency and support agriculture research that benefits United States agribusinesses and universities.",

                },
```

## API Data: fullTexts

```json
{
  "fullTexts": [
    {
      "fetchError": null,
      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/hr/BILLS-119hr4ih.xml",
      "text": "I\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 4\nIN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES\nJune 6, 2025 Mr. Scalise introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Appropriations\nA BILL\nTo rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974.\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are permanently rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $168,837,230 are permanently rescinded.\n**(3)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $203,328,007 are permanently rescinded.\n**(4)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the second paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $400,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of State\u2014Migration and Refugee Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $800,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading United States Agency for International Development\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Operating Expenses made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(17)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Transition Initiatives made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $57,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(18)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014Inter-American Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $27,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(19)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014United States African Development Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $22,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(20)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Related Programs\u2014United States Institute of Peace made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $15,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(21)**\n**(A)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2026 by Public Law 118\u201347 are hereby permanently rescinded.\n**(B)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2027 by Public Law 119\u20134 are hereby permanently rescinded.",
      "versionDate": "2025-06-06",
      "versionType": "Introduced in House"
    },

      "fetchError": null,
      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/hr/BILLS-119hr4rfs.xml",
      "text": "IIB\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 4\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nJuly 10, 2025 Received; read twice and referred jointly pursuant to the order of January 30, 1975, as amended by the order of April 11, 1986 to the committee on Appropriations and the Budget\nAN ACT\nTo rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974.\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are permanently rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $168,837,230 are permanently rescinded.\n**(3)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $203,328,007 are permanently rescinded.\n**(4)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the second paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $400,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of State\u2014Migration and Refugee Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $800,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading United States Agency for International Development\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Operating Expenses made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(17)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Transition Initiatives made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $57,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(18)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014Inter-American Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $27,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(19)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014United States African Development Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $22,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(20)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Related Programs\u2014United States Institute of Peace made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $15,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(21)**\n**(A)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2026 by Public Law 118\u201347 are hereby permanently rescinded.\n**(B)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2027 by Public Law 119\u20134 are hereby permanently rescinded.",
      "versionDate": "2025-07-10",
      "versionType": "Referred in Senate"
    },
    {

      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/hr/BILLS-119hr4pcs.xml",
      "text": "II\nCalendar No. 114\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 4\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nJuly 10, 2025 Received; read twice and referred jointly pursuant to the order of January 30, 1975, as amended by the order of April 11, 1986 to the committee on Appropriations and the Budget\nJuly 15, 2025 Discharged, by petition, pursuant to section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Act of 1974 from the Committees on Appropriations and the Budget and placed on the calendar\nAN ACT\nTo rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974.\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are permanently rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the second paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $400,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading United States Agency for International Development\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Operating Expenses made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(17)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Transition Initiatives made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $57,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(18)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014Inter-American Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $27,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(19)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014United States African Development Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $22,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(20)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Related Programs\u2014United States Institute of Peace made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $15,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(21)**\n**(A)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2026 by Public Law 118\u201347 are hereby permanently rescinded.\n**(B)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2027 by Public Law 119\u20134 are hereby permanently rescinded.",

      "text": "I\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 4\nIN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES\nAN ACT\nTo rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974.\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are permanently rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $168,837,230 are permanently rescinded.\n**(3)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Department of State, heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the second paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $400,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of State\u2014Migration and Refugee Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $800,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are permanently rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading       "text": "That the bill from the House of Representatives (H.R. 4) entitled An Act to rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974. , do pass with the following\nStrike all after the enacting clause and insert the following:\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $168,837,230 are rescinded.\n**(3)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $203,328,007 are rescinded.\n**(4)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the amounts rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for the following programs: HIV/AIDS, Tuberculosis, Malaria, Nutrition, or Maternal and Child Health: Provided further, That the preceding proviso does not apply to family planning and reproductive health programs.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of State\u2014Migration and Refugee Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $800,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for assistance to Jordan, Egypt, or the Countering PRC Influence Fund.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for Feed the Future Innovation Labs or the Countering PRC Influence Fund: Provided further, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall affect the administration of United States commodity-based food aid, including the Food for Peace program and the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded shall affect the administration of United States commodity-based food aid, including the Food for Peace program and the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading United States Agency for International Development\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Operating Expenses made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Transition Initiatives made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $57,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(17)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014Inter-American Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $27,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(18)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014United States African Development Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $22,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(19)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Related Programs\u2014United States Institute of Peace made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $15,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(20)**\n**(A)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2026 by Public Law 118\u201347 are rescinded.\n**(B)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2027 by Public Law 119\u20134 are rescinded.",
      "versionDate": "2025-07-17",
      "versionType": "EAS"
    },
    {
      "fetchError": null,
      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/hr/BILLS-119hr4enr.xml",
      "text": "I\nOne Hundred Nineteenth Congress of the United States of America\nAt the First Session\nBegun and held at the City of Washington on Friday, the third day of January, two thousand and twenty-five\nH. R. 4\nAN ACT\nTo rescind certain budget authority proposed to be rescinded in special messages transmitted to the Congress by the President on June 3, 2025, in accordance with section 1012(a) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974.\n#### 1. Short Title\nThis Act may be cited as the Rescissions Act of 2025 .\n#### 2. Rescissions of budget authority\n##### (a) In general\nPursuant to the special message transmitted by the President on June 3, 2025, to the House of Representatives and the Senate proposing the rescission of budget authority under section 1012 of part B of title X of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 682 et seq. ), the rescissions described under subsection (b) shall take effect immediately upon the date of enactment of this Act.\n##### (b) Rescissions\nThe rescissions described in this subsection are as follows:\n**(1)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $33,008,764 are rescinded.\n**(2)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions to International Organizations made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $168,837,230 are rescinded.\n**(3)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), $203,328,007 are rescinded.\n**(4)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading International Organizations\u2014Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $157,906,000 are rescinded.\n**(5)**\nOf the unobligated balances in the first paragraph under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Global Health Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $500,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the amounts rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for the following programs: HIV/AIDS, Tuberculosis, Malaria, Nutrition, or Maternal and Child Health: Provided further, That the preceding proviso does not apply to family planning and reproductive health programs.\n**(6)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Department of State\u2014Migration and Refugee Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $800,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(7)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Complex Crises Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $43,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(8)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Democracy Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $83,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(9)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Economic Support Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $1,650,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for assistance to Jordan, Egypt, or the Countering PRC Influence Fund.\n**(10)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014International Financial Institutions\u2014Contribution to the Clean Technology Fund made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(11)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Multilateral Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Organizations and Programs made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $436,920,000 are rescinded.\n**(12)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Development Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $2,500,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall be from the unobligated balances for Feed the Future Innovation Labs or the Countering PRC Influence Fund: Provided further, That none of the funds rescinded under this paragraph shall affect the administration of United States commodity-based food aid, including the Food for Peace program and the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program.\n**(13)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $460,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(14)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014International Disaster Assistance made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $496,000,000 are rescinded: Provided, That none of the funds rescinded shall affect the administration of United States commodity-based food aid, including the Food for Peace program and the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program.\n**(15)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading United States Agency for International Development\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Operating Expenses made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $125,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(16)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Funds Appropriated to the President\u2014Transition Initiatives made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $57,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(17)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014Inter-American Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $27,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(18)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Bilateral Economic Assistance\u2014Independent Agencies\u2014United States African Development Foundation made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $22,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(19)**\nOf the unobligated balances under the heading Related Programs\u2014United States Institute of Peace made available by the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 (division A of Public Law 119\u20134 ), $15,000,000 are rescinded.\n**(20)**\n**(A)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2026 by Public Law 118\u201347 are rescinded.\n**(B)**\nAmounts made available for Corporation for Public Broadcasting for fiscal year 2027 by Public Law 119\u20134 are rescinded.",
```

## API Data: summaries

```json
{
  "govinfoBulk": {
    "introducedDate": "2025-06-06",
    "originChamber": "House",
    "payload": {
      "dublinCore": {
        "contributor": "Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress",
        "description": "This file contains bill summaries for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is \"to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives\". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.",
        "format": "text/xml",
        "language": "EN",
        "rights": "Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain."
      },
      "item": {
        "@attributes": {
          "congress": "119",
          "measure-id": "id119hr4",
          "measure-number": "4",
          "measure-type": "hr",
          "orig-publish-date": "2025-06-06",
          "originChamber": "HOUSE",
          "update-date": "2025-06-13"
        },
        "summary": [
          {
            "@attributes": {
              "currentChamber": "HOUSE",
              "summary-id": "id119hr4v53",
              "update-date": "2025-06-13"
            },
            "action-date": "2025-06-12",
            "action-desc": "Passed House",
            "summary-text": "<p><strong>Rescissions Act of 2025</strong></p><p>This bill rescinds $9.4 billion in\u00a0unobligated funds that were provided to the Department of State, the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), various independent and related agencies, and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\u00a0</p><p>The rescissions were proposed by the President under procedures included in the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974. Under current law, the President may propose rescissions to Congress using specified procedures, and the rescissions must be enacted into law to take effect.\u00a0</p><p>Specifically, the bill rescinds funds that were provided to the State Department or the President for</p><ul><li>Contributions to International Organizations;</li><li>Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities;</li><li>Global Health Programs;</li><li>Migration and Refugee Assistance;</li><li>the Complex Crises Fund;</li><li>the Democracy Fund;</li><li>the Economic Support Fund;</li><li>Contributions to the Clean Technology Fund;</li><li>International Organization and Programs;</li><li>Development Assistance;</li><li>Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia;</li><li>International Disaster Assistance; and</li><li>Transition Initiatives.</li></ul><p>The bill also rescinds funds that were provided for\u00a0</p><ul><li>USAID Operating Expenses,</li><li>the Inter-American Foundation,</li><li>the\u00a0U.S. African Development Foundation,</li><li>the U.S. Institute of Peace, and</li><li>the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.</li></ul>"
          },
          {
            "@attributes": {
              "currentChamber": "HOUSE",
              "summary-id": "id119hr4v00",
              "update-date": "2025-06-13"
            },
            "action-date": "2025-06-06",
            "action-desc": "Introduced in House",
            "summary-text": "<p><strong>Rescissions Act of 2025</strong></p><p>This bill rescinds $9.4 billion in\u00a0unobligated funds that were provided to the Department of State, the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), various independent and related agencies, and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\u00a0</p><p>The rescissions were proposed by the President under procedures included in the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974. Under current law, the President may propose rescissions to Congress using specified procedures, and the rescissions must be enacted into law to take effect.\u00a0</p><p>Specifically, the bill rescinds funds that were provided to the State Department or the President for</p><ul><li>Contributions to International Organizations;</li><li>Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities;</li><li>Global Health Programs;</li><li>Migration and Refugee Assistance;</li><li>the Complex Crises Fund;</li><li>the Democracy Fund;</li><li>the Economic Support Fund;</li><li>Contributions to the Clean Technology Fund;</li><li>International Organization and Programs;</li><li>Development Assistance;</li><li>Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia;</li><li>International Disaster Assistance; and</li><li>Transition Initiatives.</li></ul><p>The bill also rescinds funds that were provided for\u00a0</p><ul><li>USAID Operating Expenses,</li><li>the Inter-American Foundation,</li><li>the\u00a0U.S. African Development Foundation,</li><li>the U.S. Institute of Peace, and</li><li>the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.</li></ul>"
          }
        ],
        "title": "Rescissions Act of 2025"
      }
    },
    "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLSUM/119/hr/BILLSUM-119hr4.xml",
    "summary": {
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",

      "text": "<p><strong>Rescissions Act of 2025</strong></p><p>This bill rescinds $9.4 billion in\u00a0unobligated funds that were provided to the Department of State, the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), various independent and related agencies, and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\u00a0</p><p>The rescissions were proposed by the President under procedures included in the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974. Under current law, the President may propose rescissions to Congress using specified procedures, and the rescissions must be enacted into law to take effect.\u00a0</p><p>Specifically, the bill rescinds funds that were provided to the State Department or the President for</p><ul><li>Contributions to International Organizations;</li><li>Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities;</li><li>Global Health Programs;</li><li>Migration and Refugee Assistance;</li><li>the Complex Crises Fund;</li><li>the Democracy Fund;</li><li>the Economic Support Fund;</li><li>Contributions to the Clean Technology Fund;</li><li>International Organization and Programs;</li><li>Development Assistance;</li><li>Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia;</li><li>International Disaster Assistance; and</li><li>Transition Initiatives.</li></ul><p>The bill also rescinds funds that were provided for\u00a0</p><ul><li>USAID Operating Expenses,</li><li>the Inter-American Foundation,</li><li>the\u00a0U.S. African Development Foundation,</li><li>the U.S. Institute of Peace, and</li><li>the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.</li></ul>",
      "updateDate": "2025-06-13",
      "versionCode": "id119hr4"
    },
    "title": "Rescissions Act of 2025"
  },
  "summaries": [
    {
      "actionDate": "2025-06-12",
      "actionDesc": "Passed House",
      "text": "<p><strong>Rescissions Act of 2025</strong></p><p>This bill rescinds $9.4 billion in\u00a0unobligated funds that were provided to the Department of State, the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), various independent and related agencies, and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\u00a0</p><p>The rescissions were proposed by the President under procedures included in the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974. Under current law, the President may propose rescissions to Congress using specified procedures, and the rescissions must be enacted into law to take effect.\u00a0</p><p>Specifically, the bill rescinds funds that were provided to the State Department or the President for</p><ul><li>Contributions to International Organizations;</li><li>Contributions for International Peacekeeping Activities;</li><li>Global Health Programs;</li><li>Migration and Refugee Assistance;</li><li>the Complex Crises Fund;</li><li>the Democracy Fund;</li><li>the Economic Support Fund;</li><li>Contributions to the Clean Technology Fund;</li><li>International Organization and Programs;</li><li>Development Assistance;</li><li>Assistance for Europe, Eurasia, and Central Asia;</li><li>International Disaster Assistance; and</li><li>Transition Initiatives.</li></ul><p>The bill also rescinds funds that were provided for\u00a0</p><ul><li>USAID Operating Expenses,</li><li>the Inter-American Foundation,</li><li>the\u00a0U.S. African Development Foundation,</li><li>the U.S. Institute of Peace, and</li><li>the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.</li></ul>",
      "updateDate": "2025-06-13",
      "versionCode": "id119hr4"
    }
  ]
}
```
